description_long: This is a flat, irregularly shaped stone with a dark gray to nearly black coloration and a slightly glossy, uneven surface. The stone appears to be thin and roughly triangular, with jagged edges and a somewhat pointed tip at one end. Subtle surface undulations and minor pitting are visible, suggesting natural weathering rather than artificial shaping. The stone's surface shows faint lighter streaks and patches, possibly from mineral inclusions or exposure to the elements. A white marking, possibly an identification number, is visible on one face. The overall appearance is that of a naturally occurring stone, likely sourced from an outdoor environment.
description: A thin, dark gray stone with jagged edges, subtle surface undulations, and a slightly glossy finish. White markings are visible on one face.
description_view_0: The stone appears pointed and roughly triangular, with the white marking visible near the center of the upper surface.
description_view_1: From the side, the stone looks very thin and flat, with a slight curvature and irregular edge profile.
description_view_2: The opposite end is more rounded and blunt, with the white marking still partially visible and the surface texture more apparent.
description_view_3: This side view highlights the stone's flatness and the subtle undulations on its surface, with a slight taper toward one end.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a ground-level obstacle
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and faint streaks, with no regular or artificial patterning; the surface is mostly natural with minor mineral inclusions.
attribute_weathering: Slightly weathered with minor pitting and surface undulations, indicating moderate exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural ground surfaces or contrasts with paved urban environments; may be partially embedded or resting atop soil or gravel.
